NanoString & tCycIF patients (n=30)

Speaker notes:
The FINPROVE trial as a whole includes roughly 900 patients, covering many different cancer types.
From these, 137 are gynecological cancer patients.

From the gynecological patients, we selected a smaller subset for NanoString and tCycIF. We included patients with LGSC histology or patients who had been treated with a MEK inhibitor

Nanostring panel

4.5.2026

Esittäjä

7

Speaker notes:
-the panel that we used is the NanoString pancancer io 360
-consists of 770 genes across 16 different immuno-oncology pathways
-
 -it was missing some crucial genes that we wanted to look at
 -to make it more suitable for our objectives, we decided to add 54 extra genes to the panel
 -the additional genes are all related to the MAPK pathway somehow 
 -
-

Speaker notes:
-the panel that we used is the NanoString pancancer io 360
-consists of 770 genes across 16 different immuno-oncology pathways
-
 -it was missing some crucial genes that we wanted to look at
 -to make it more suitable for our objectives, we decided to add 54 extra genes to the panel
 -the additional genes are all related to the MAPK pathway somehow

Extra genes in the nanostring panel

Selected biologically relevant genes based on MAPK signaling and MEK inhibitor response

And as you can tell, we have quite a poor amount of significant gene hits in this analysis compared to our other analyses

IN ORDER TO GET RELIABLE RESULTS WE NEED MORE DATA ON THE LGSC PATIENTS (especially those who have received treatment)

Antibody panel

4.5.2026

Esittäjä

14

8 cycles

25 markers
two MAPK-signaling markers are the main focus


two failed markers
pJNK 
CD206

Speaker notes:
- The main focus in this project is the functional markers pCREB and pERK


what are pERK's and pCREB's roles in the MEK-pathway?
these are the most likely ones to activate when patients have mutations in the MAPK-pathway -> this way we can identify the patients with the activated MAPK-pathways

Speaker notes:
- The main focus in this project is the functional markers pCREB and pERK


what are pERK's and pCREB's roles in the MEK-pathway?
these are the most likely ones to activate when patients have mutations in the MAPK-pathway -> this way we can identify the patients with the activated MAPK-pathways

Samples separate into high and low pERK and pCREB expression groups, with intra-sample heterogeneity.
